description: VitalDB (Vital Signs DataBase), Seoul National University Hospital. 6,388 surgical cases. Three data layers: (1) high-resolution intraoperative waveform/numeric tracks (~196 parameters across 11 devices), (2) a per-case clinical-information table (~74 parameters: demographics, surgery/anesthesia, intraoperative drugs/fluids/transfusion, and IN-HOSPITAL outcomes), and (3) a perioperative laboratory time-series (34 blood tests) spanning 90 days before to 90 days after anesthesia start. Single-center. The follow-up horizon is the index hospitalization plus a +/-90-day lab window around surgery; there is NO structured ward assessment (delirium/cognition/pain), NO post-discharge vital-status follow-up beyond in-hospital, NO readmission tracking, and NO biospecimens/genetics.

scope.lab_window: -90 to +90 days around anesthesia start (postoperative labs ARE included)
scope.source: Lee et al., Scientific Data 2022 (s41597-022-01411-5); PhysioNet vitaldb 1.0.0 lab_parameters.csv

variables.name: BIS (Bispectral Index)
variables.missingness: Device-dependent: present only for cases monitored with a BIS Vista module (a subset of cases). Gaps during electrocautery/probe repositioning; absent pre-induction and post-emergence.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming every case has BIS. Filter by track availability and power on the BIS subset, not all 6,388 cases.
variables.notes: Companion tracks: BIS/SR, BIS/EEG, BIS/SEF, BIS/EMG, BIS/TOTPOW, BIS/SQI.

variables.name: Suppression Ratio (burst suppression)
variables.missingness: Available only when a BIS module was used (same subset as BIS). Derived EEG metric, not raw-EEG adjudication; sparse relative to raw EEG.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating SR>0 as a validated burst-suppression label, and (critically) assuming a downstream NEUROLOGIC OUTCOME exists to relate it to. The exposure is here; ward outcomes like delirium/POCD are not.
variables.notes: Exposure in the canonical 'burst suppression -> postoperative delirium' question; exposure present, outcome absent.

variables.name: Raw EEG (BIS channel)
variables.missingness: Single/limited-channel processed-EEG from the BIS module only; not full multichannel clinical EEG. BIS-subset only.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting montage-grade multichannel EEG; this is the BIS device channel.

variables.name: Arterial pressure waveform (invasive)
variables.missingness: Present only for cases with an arterial line (higher-acuity surgery). Many minor/ambulatory cases have only intermittent NIBP. Damping/flush artifacts.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming arterial waveforms exist for all cases; they concentrate in major surgery.

variables.name: ECG waveform
variables.missingness: Generally present intraoperatively; lead noise/disconnect gaps. Lead II primarily.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting diagnostic 12-lead ECG; this is monitoring-lead continuous ECG.

variables.name: Photoplethysmogram (PPG)
variables.missingness: Fingertip PPG; motion/low-perfusion artifact. Present for most cases.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating PPG amplitude as calibrated; it is relative and device/probe dependent.

variables.name: Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP)
variables.missingness: Invasive MBP needs an arterial line (~1-2 s); non-invasive MBP is intermittent (every few min). Choose the source matching your hypotension definition.
variables.common_mistakes: Mixing invasive and cuff MAP sources with different sampling rates/bias, producing an inconsistent hypotension exposure.

variables.name: Systolic / diastolic blood pressure
variables.missingness: Invasive vs non-invasive sources differ in cadence and availability as with MAP.
variables.common_mistakes: Conflating invasive and cuff systolic/diastolic series.

variables.name: Heart Rate (HR)
variables.missingness: Well populated intraoperatively; brief ECG-artifact gaps.
variables.common_mistakes: Confusing monitor HR with PPG-derived pulse rate during artifact.

variables.name: Peripheral oxygen saturation (SpO2)
variables.missingness: Well populated intraoperatively; motion/low-perfusion artifact causes spurious drops.
variables.common_mistakes: Counting artifactual transient drops as desaturation without artifact rejection.

variables.name: End-tidal CO2 (EtCO2)
variables.missingness: Valid only during the intubated/ventilated window; absent before intubation and after extubation.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming a continuous EtCO2 trace across the whole case.

variables.name: Respiratory rate
variables.missingness: Ventilator-set vs measured RR differ; meaningful mainly during controlled ventilation.
variables.common_mistakes: Mixing set and spontaneous RR.

variables.name: Body temperature
variables.missingness: Present when a temperature probe was used; site (esophageal/nasopharyngeal/skin) not always specified and coverage is partial.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating all temperature traces as core temperature regardless of probe site.

variables.name: Tidal volume (ventilator)
variables.missingness: Only during mechanical ventilation from the anesthesia machine; absent for spontaneous/mask cases and outside the ventilated window.
variables.common_mistakes: Using tidal volume as if it spans the whole case.

variables.name: Airway pressure (peak/PEEP/plateau)
variables.missingness: Ventilated window only; machine-track dependent.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming driving pressure is provided directly; it must be derived (plateau - PEEP).

variables.name: Inspired/expired volatile anesthetic concentration
variables.missingness: Volatile-anesthetic cases only; TIVA (propofol) cases have no agent gas concentration.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting volatile concentrations for TIVA cases; use Orchestra TCI tracks there instead.

variables.name: Inspired oxygen fraction (FiO2)
variables.missingness: Anesthesia-machine ventilated window only.
variables.common_mistakes: Confusing inspired vs expired O2 tracks.

variables.name: Cardiac output / index
variables.missingness: Device-dependent and present only for cases monitored with an advanced hemodynamic monitor (a small subset). Different devices/algorithms are not interchangeable.
variables.common_mistakes: Pooling CO across EV1000/Vigileo/CardioQ as if calibrated identically; and assuming most cases have any CO monitor (few do).

variables.name: Stroke volume variation / fluid responsiveness indices
variables.missingness: Advanced-monitor subset only.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming SVV validity outside controlled ventilation / fixed tidal volume conditions.

variables.name: Cerebral oxygen saturation (NIRS)
variables.missingness: INVOS device subset only (selected cases, e.g. cardiac/major surgery).
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming broad availability; NIRS is used in a minority of cases.

variables.name: Target-controlled infusion concentrations (propofol/remifentanil)
variables.missingness: Orchestra pump subset (TIVA/TCI cases). Plasma (CP) and effect-site (CE) are model estimates, not measured concentrations.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating modeled effect-site concentration as a measured drug level.

variables.name: Age
variables.missingness: Near-complete per-case column. Adult-dominant cohort; limited pediatric coverage.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming wide pediatric range.

variables.name: Sex
variables.missingness: Near-complete. Recorded natal sex.
variables.common_mistakes: Interpreting as gender identity.

variables.name: Height
variables.missingness: Mostly complete.
variables.common_mistakes: None major; pair with weight for BMI.

variables.name: Weight
variables.missingness: Mostly complete.
variables.common_mistakes: None major.

variables.name: Body mass index
variables.missingness: Provided per case (also derivable from height/weight).
variables.common_mistakes: Using BMI as a proxy for body composition in a single-center Korean cohort without considering distribution differences.

variables.name: ASA physical status classification
variables.missingness: Mostly complete; skews ASA 1-3 (elective population).
variables.common_mistakes: Treating ASA as a precise severity score.

variables.name: Emergency surgery flag
variables.missingness: Per-case flag; emergency cases are a minority.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming a graded urgency scale; this is a binary flag.

variables.name: Type of anesthesia
variables.missingness: Per-case category (e.g. general, spinal/regional).
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming TIVA vs volatile is directly coded here; infer from drug/agent tracks too.

variables.name: Surgery type / operation / department / diagnosis
variables.missingness: 'optype' coarse category, 'opname'/'dx' free text, 'department' surgical service. Single-center case mix; some categories sparse.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming standardized coding (CPT/ICD-PCS); harmonization of free-text fields is manual.

variables.name: Surgical approach and patient position
variables.missingness: Per-case categories (e.g. open/laparoscopic; supine/prone/lateral).
variables.common_mistakes: Sparse categories for uncommon approaches/positions.

variables.name: Estimated blood loss
variables.missingness: Clinician-estimated case total, not a time series; known to be imprecise.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating EBL as an accurate continuous measurement.

variables.name: Intraoperative urine output
variables.missingness: Case total; recorded mainly when a urinary catheter was placed.
variables.common_mistakes: Using intraoperative UO as a renal-outcome endpoint; pair with postoperative creatinine for AKI.

variables.name: Intraoperative transfusion (RBC/FFP)
variables.missingness: Case totals (units). Platelets/cryoprecipitate not separately columned.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming all blood-product types are itemized; only RBC and FFP are.

variables.name: Intraoperative crystalloid/colloid volume
variables.missingness: Case totals, not time-resolved infusion curves.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting time-stamped fluid boluses; only totals are provided.

variables.name: Intraoperative vasopressor/inotrope bolus dose
variables.missingness: Case-total bolus doses; continuous infusions of some agents may instead appear in Orchestra pump tracks.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating bolus-dose totals as continuous infusion exposure.

variables.name: Intraoperative anesthetic/relaxant bolus dose
variables.missingness: Case-total bolus doses. TCI/continuous propofol/remifentanil appear in Orchestra tracks, not here.
variables.common_mistakes: Summing bolus totals with TCI exposure without reconciling the two sources.

variables.name: Preoperative comorbidity flags (HTN/DM/arrhythmia/PFT)
variables.missingness: Limited comorbidity set only (hypertension, diabetes, ECG arrhythmia, pulmonary function). No structured Charlson/full problem list.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting a complete comorbidity index; only these few flags exist, so confounder adjustment is limited.

variables.name: Preoperative laboratory panel
variables.vitaldb_track: clinical_info.preop_* (hb, plt, pt, aptt, na, k, gluc, alb, ast, alt, bun, cr, ph, hco3, be, pao2, paco2, sao2)
variables.missingness: Per-case preoperative values; some labs missing when not ordered. Baseline creatinine (preop_cr) available for AKI baselines.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming every preop lab is present for every case.

variables.name: Perioperative laboratory time-series (34 tests, -90 to +90 days)
variables.vitaldb_track: lab_data (name/dt/result): wbc, hb, hct, plt, esr, gluc, tprot, alb, tbil, ast, alt, bun, cr, gfr, ccr, na, k, ica, cl, ammo, crp, lac, ptinr, pt%, ptsec, aptt, fib, ph, pco2, po2, hco3, be, sao2
variables.missingness: Irregular, order-driven sampling (NOT fixed schedule). Window is -90 to +90 days around anesthesia start, so POSTOPERATIVE labs exist but only when clinically ordered; many cases have few or no postop draws. Coverage thins with distance from surgery.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming dense, scheduled postoperative labs. Sampling is sparse and informative-missing (sicker patients get more draws); a postop value's ABSENCE is not random.

variables.name: Postoperative creatinine (for AKI)
variables.vitaldb_track: lab_data name='cr' (+ baseline clinical_info.preop_cr; gfr/ccr also available)
variables.missingness: Present within the +/-90-day lab window BUT only when creatinine was ordered postoperatively. Many cases lack postop draws, so an AKI cohort is a SELECTED subset, not all 6,388.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming KDIGO-grade AKI ascertainment for everyone. You can DERIVE an AKI endpoint from pre/post creatinine where draws exist, but there is no pre-labeled AKI field and the urine-output KDIGO criterion is not reliably available postoperatively.

variables.name: In-hospital mortality
variables.missingness: Captures death during the index hospitalization only. Events are rare (low base rate) -> limited power for mortality models.
variables.common_mistakes: Equating in-hospital mortality with 30-day or post-discharge mortality; a patient who dies after discharge is recorded as alive. Use in_hospital_mortality only for in-hospital death endpoints.

variables.name: ICU length of stay
variables.missingness: Postoperative ICU days. Zero for non-ICU cases; right-skewed.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating ICU days as ICU admission yes/no without checking zeros, or as a quality metric without case-mix adjustment.

variables.name: Hospital length of stay
variables.vitaldb_track: derived from clinical_info.adm and clinical_info.dis (admission/discharge times)
variables.missingness: Not a pre-computed column; compute from discharge minus admission. Single-center discharge practices.
variables.common_mistakes: Expecting a ready-made LOS field; it must be derived from adm/dis timestamps.

variables.name: Postoperative delirium
variables.missingness: Not collected. Delirium requires structured ward assessments (CAM/CAM-ICU/3D-CAM, DSM-5) over postoperative days 1-5. VitalDB has no ward-course or delirium-screening data; its postoperative footprint is lab draws and a few in-hospital outcome fields only.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming an intraoperative EEG/BIS signal substitutes for a delirium label. Burst suppression is an intraoperative EXPOSURE; delirium is a structured ward OUTCOME VitalDB does not record. The study cannot be done in VitalDB alone.
variables.notes: Canonical trustworthy 'no' for the burst-suppression -> delirium question.

variables.name: Postoperative cognitive dysfunction (POCD)
variables.missingness: Not collected. POCD requires baseline and follow-up neuropsychological testing (weeks-months postop). VitalDB has no cognitive testing and no post-discharge follow-up.
variables.common_mistakes: Conflating intraoperative depth-of-anesthesia signals with a downstream cognitive outcome that is never ascertained here.

variables.name: 30-day (post-discharge) mortality
variables.missingness: Not collected as specified. VitalDB records IN-HOSPITAL mortality (death_inhosp) only; there is no post-discharge vital-status follow-up or death-registry linkage, so a patient discharged alive then dying by day 30 is not captured.
variables.common_mistakes: Using in-hospital mortality as if it were 30-day mortality. They differ materially (short LOS misses post-discharge deaths). If your endpoint truly requires 30-day vital status, it is NOT feasible; if in-hospital death suffices, use 'in_hospital_mortality' instead.

variables.name: Hospital readmission
variables.missingness: Not collected. Readmission requires post-discharge encounter tracking. VitalDB ends at the index hospitalization with no longitudinal encounter history.
variables.common_mistakes: Treating a single-encounter dataset as if it held the longitudinal admission history needed for readmission.

variables.name: Postoperative pain scores (ward)
variables.missingness: Not collected. Routine ward pain scores (NRS/VAS over postop days) are not part of the clinical-information table or any track.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming a perioperative dataset includes ward pain assessments; these structured nursing scores are absent.

variables.name: Postoperative nausea and vomiting (ward)
variables.missingness: Not collected. PONV is a structured ward outcome not recorded in VitalDB.
variables.common_mistakes: Inferring PONV from intraoperative antiemetic dosing; that is exposure, not outcome ascertainment.

variables.name: Genetic / genomic data
variables.missingness: Not collected. No biospecimens, genotyping, sequencing, or pharmacogenomic data of any kind.
variables.common_mistakes: Assuming a rich physiologic dataset also carries molecular/genetic covariates. Any gene-environment or pharmacogenomic question is out of scope.
